1: vayashov hamalakh haddover bi vaye'ireni kish asher ye'ur mishshenato: 'And the angel who talked with me came again and woke me, like a man who is awakened out of his sleep.' The fifth vision: the golden lampstand and the two olive trees. Zechariah is awakened from what appears to be a sleep between visions — the transition from one visionary state to another is described as waking. The angel who 'woke him like a man woken from sleep' suggests the depth of the visionary state: prophetic trance, not ordinary wakefulness.
2: vayomer elai mah atta ro'eh vaomar ra'iti vehineh menorah zahav kulloh ugelah al roshshah veshiv'ah neroteiha aleha shiv'ah veshiv'ah mutzaqot lanerot asher al roshhah: 'And he said to me, What do you see? I said, I see, and behold, a lampstand all of gold, with a bowl on the top of it, and seven lamps on it, with seven lips on each of the lamps that are on the top of it.' The golden lampstand (menorah zahav) echoes the tabernacle/temple menorah (Exodus 25:31-40; Zechariah 37:17-24) but with additions: the bowl (gulah = basin, H1543) on top serves as a reservoir fed by the two olive trees (v11-12). The seven lamps with seven lips each = forty-nine channels for oil. The elaborate oil-supply system represents the inexhaustibility of divine supply for the light.
3: ushne zeitim aleha echad mimin hagelah ve'echad al smolah: 'And there are two olive trees by it, one on the right of the bowl and the other on its left.' The two olive trees (shne zeitim) feed the lampstand with oil without human intervention. They are identified in v14 as 'the two anointed ones' (benei hayitshar = the two sons of fresh oil) who stand beside the Lord of all the earth. The two figures are Zerubbabel (the Davidic prince/civil leader) and Joshua (the high priest) — the two anointed offices of king and priest that will be ultimately united in the messianic priest-king (Zechariah 6:12-13).
4: va'an veomer el hamalakh haddover bi leimor mah eleh Adonai: 'And I said to the angel who talked with me, What are these, my lord?' The interpretive dialogue structure: vision → question → interpretation. Zechariah's question opens the theological unpacking of the lampstand vision. The angel's counter-question ('Don't you know what these are?') adds rhetorical emphasis — this is important enough that the prophet should recognize its significance before the explanation is given.
5: vaya'an hamalakh haddover bi vayomer elai halo yadaa'ta mah eleh vaomar lo Adonai: 'Then the angel who talked with me answered and said to me, Do you not know what these are? I said, No, my lord.' The prophet's honest acknowledgment of ignorance creates the space for revelation. The visionary dialogue is a Socratic method: the angel's question ('don't you know?') implies the prophet should know — or will know — and the exchange creates the teaching moment. This dialogue structure is characteristic of both Zechariah and Daniel's visions.
6: vaya'an vayomer elai leimor zeh davar YHWH el Zerubavel leimor lo bechayil velo bekoach ki im beruchi amar YHWH tzvaot: 'Then he said to me, This is the word of the LORD to Zerubbabel: Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the LORD of hosts.' The central theological declaration of the vision. Lo bechayil velo bekoach ki im beruchi — three contrasting terms: chayil (H2428 — military force, army, strength), koach (H3581 — physical power, ability), and ruach (H7307 — Spirit, breath, wind). The temple will not be rebuilt by human military or physical capacity but by the divine Spirit who sustains all legitimate work. This principle is the OT's most concentrated statement of divine-Spirit dependency, applied to Zerubbabel's task of temple rebuilding.
7: mi atta har hagadol lifnei Zerubavel lemishor vehotzi et ha'aven haroshshah tehshotah tehshotah: 'Who are you, O great mountain? Before Zerubbabel you shall become a plain. And he shall bring forward the top stone amid shouts of Grace, grace to it!' The 'great mountain' (har hagadol) is the obstacle to temple completion — whether political opposition, resource shortage, or spiritual resistance. YHWH commands it to become a plain (lemishor) before Zerubbabel. The capstone (ha'aven haroshshah, the top stone, the finishing stone of the building) will be brought forward with shouts of chen chen lah (grace, grace to it!) — the community's acclaim when the temple is completed (cf. Ezra 6:15-16).
8: vayehi davar YHWH elai leimor: 'Moreover, the word of the LORD came to me, saying.' A second oracle within the vision, confirming Zerubbabel's role. The double oracle (the vision interpretation plus this additional word) underscores the importance of the promise about Zerubbabel and the temple completion.
9: yedei Zerubavel yisdu habayit hazeh veyedav tevatzzenah veyada'ta ki YHWH tzvaot shelachani aleikhem: 'The hands of Zerubbabel have laid the foundation of this house; his hands shall also complete it. Then you will know that the LORD of hosts has sent me to you.' The same hands that laid the foundation (520 BCE) will complete the building — a divine guarantee of continuity and completion. The authentication formula ('you will know that YHWH sent me') appears repeatedly in Zechariah's visions: the completion of the temple will vindicate the prophetic word.
10: ki mi vaz leyom qetannot veshamechu vera'u et ha'eben habedil beyad Zerubavel shiv'ah eleh einei YHWH hemah meshottim bekhol ha'aretz: 'For whoever has despised the day of small things shall rejoice, and shall see the plumb line in the hand of Zerubbabel. These seven are the eyes of the LORD, which range through the whole earth.' 'The day of small things' (yom qetannot) — the modest, seemingly inadequate beginnings of the second temple. Those who despise the small beginning will yet rejoice when they see completion. The seven eyes (shiv'ah eleh einei YHWH) are YHWH's omniscient surveillance of the whole earth — corresponding to the seven eyes of the stone in Zechariah 3:9, and the seven lampstand lamps. Seven = completeness of divine knowledge and oversight.
11: va'an veomer elav mah shene hazeitim ha'eleh al yemim hamenorah veal smolah: 'Then I said to him, What are these two olive trees on the right and the left of the lampstand?' The question about the two olive trees whose identity has been held back opens the final interpretation of the vision. The two trees that continuously supply oil without human intervention are the key to understanding the vision's theological content.
12: va'an shenith veomer elav mah shete shibelei hazeitim asher beyad shene tzanterot hazahav hameriiqim me'aleihem hazahav: 'And a second time I answered and said to him, What are these two branches of the olive trees, which are beside the two golden pipes from which the golden oil is poured out?' Zechariah's precise observation: branches (shibelei, H7641 = ears of grain/branches, figurative for the olive-branch tips) beside golden pipes (tzanterot, H6804 — channels, tubes) through which golden oil flows. The botanical precision of the vision is remarkable: it describes the vascular system of the olive-oil supply. The golden pipes = the channels of divine Spirit-supply.
13: vayomer elai leimor halo yadaa'ta mah eleh vaomar lo Adonai: 'He said to me, Do you not know what these are? I said, No, my lord.' The repeated pattern of ignorance acknowledged — the prophet genuinely does not know, and the angel genuinely teaches. The reciprocal exchange emphasizes: the identity of the two anointed ones is not self-evident and requires divine revelation.
14: vayomer eleh shene venei hayitshar ha'omedim al Adon kol ha'aretz: 'And he said, These are the two anointed ones who stand by the Lord of the whole earth.' Benei hayitshar (sons of fresh oil, H3323) — lit. sons of oil/new oil, meaning those who are anointed with oil = anointed ones. The two anointed ones = the two office-bearers who supply the divine Spirit to the community: Zerubbabel (civil/royal) and Joshua (priestly). Together they represent the two-office system of the OT covenant community. The ultimate fulfillment: Christ as both king and priest (Hebrews 7:1-3; Revelation 1:6), the one in whom the two offices are permanently united.
